description: Located a short distance away; close in space. 'Noshi' names the quality of closeness—proximity that requires little effort to bridge, nearness that enables easy reaching.
sound_symbolism: The word stays close. 'No' grounds with present nearness, proximity established. Then 'shi' /ʃi/ settles close, nearness confirmed.
grammatical_notes: Functions as an adjective: 'noshi lokue' (a near place). Distinct from preposition 'pai' (near/at) which marks spatial relationship; this is the quality of being close. Contrast: wuero (far).

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: What is near is available—the present moment, the immediate situation, what can actually be engaged.
pillars.peace-linguistics-practices: Near presence enables connection—physical proximity supports emotional closeness.
